English: c) to be drawn up, composed as a lexicon
b) to be ordained, receive holy orders, be consecrated
Ethpa. a) to be set or arranged in order, placed, ranked with
Pass. Part. a) placed, arranged, ordered, orderly, moderate, modest, self-controlled
b) to ordain, confer holy orders; to set over, appoint a bishop; to institute, constitute, ordain laws, canons, festivals
Pa. a) to order, set or place in order, arrange, assign, to lay out a corpse
